summary: Descriptive overview of divorcegrounds in substantive law: fault vs no-fault, separation periods.
key_points: fault vs no-fault is a fundamental concept in family law.
Legal standards for divorcegrounds are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine divorcegrounds in litigation.
The doctrine of divorcegrounds has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding divorcegrounds requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
